title: Naked and semi-naked cake finish
subtitle: Using an offset spatula to create a deliberately spare frosted look
excerpt: A naked cake has no frosting on its sides; a semi-naked cake has a very thin, sheer layer that lets the sponge show through. Both finishes require a well-crumbed layer cake, a generous amount of frosting between the layers, and confident scraping with a palette knife or icing scraper.
sourceNotes: The minimal or 'naked' cake finish is a contemporary domestic baking style documented extensively in twenty-first-century baking guides. The underlying technique of applying buttercream between layers and scraping the sides clean is a standard domestic method requiring no proprietary tools. The use of an offset spatula and bench scraper for cake finishing is described in general terms in domestic baking references widely available online and in public-domain household cooking guides.

content.text: The naked finish looks deliberately spare but requires confident technique. The sponge layers must be level, the filling generous, and the scraping decisive. Hesitant passes leave uneven texture and drag frosting over the exposed sponge.

Place the first sponge layer on the turntable. Pipe or spread a thick ring of buttercream around the edge, then fill the centre. The ring acts as a dam and keeps the filling level. Place the second layer on top, press gently, and repeat. Add the third layer. If the stack leans, refrigerate for 15 minutes before proceeding.
Apply frosting to sides, then scrape
Apply a generous coat of buttercream all over the cake with the icing spatula. Do not worry about neatness. Then take the icing smoother and, using the

that suits the finish you want, rotate the turntable in one smooth motion while holding the scraper against the side. For a semi-naked finish, one firm pass is usually enough. For fully naked, repeat until the sponge is clearly visible.
Use the icing spatula to smooth the top, pulling any excess frosting from the edges inward. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before adding any decoration. Serve at room temperature.
